programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Beginner
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all.  With a focus on safe landing techniques, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard - Advanced
programs.description: In advanced music you will build on fun and fundamentals and work towards refinement. Here you will continue to practice proper hand positions and basic scales and learn more complex rhythms and the use of foot pedals. As you continue to practice you will work towards performing more complex pieces.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational with Family
programs.description: Do you like the idea of participating in physical activity with your family? Develop skills through play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction. Each individual must register for this activity. At least one child or youth with at least one adult must register together.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada's Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen. programs.name: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Beginner
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all.  With a focus on safe landing techniques, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Music: Guitar - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard - Advanced
programs.description: In advanced music you will build on fun and fundamentals and work towards refinement. Here you will continue to practice proper hand positions and basic scales and learn more complex rhythms and the use of foot pedals. As you continue to practice you will work towards performing more complex pieces.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational with Family
programs.description: Do you like the idea of participating in physical activity with your family? Develop skills through play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction. Each individual must register for this activity. At least one child or youth with at least one adult must register together.
